terms.definition: A function that assigns each vector a non-negative length, zero only at the origin, scaling linearly with scalars and satisfying the triangle inequality.
The Euclidean norm ||x||₂ = sqrt(Σ xᵢ²), measuring straight-line length in coordinate space.
The sum of absolute coordinates ||x||₁ = Σ |xᵢ|, also called Manhattan length.
A function d(u,v) measuring separation between points, usually d(u,v) = ||u - v|| for some norm.
Dot product divided by the product of L2 norms, measuring alignment of direction independent of magnitude.
